# Competitor Tracker & Co. MCP Server ★ New (July 16)

Watches your competitors' websites and tells you what changed. Every week it crawls their pricing, product, messaging, and corporate pages, detects the changes that matter, and files a tagged, ranked report — all accessible through MCP-compatible AI agents.

**Source:** mcp.so (submitted July 16, 2026)
**Category:** AI & Agents
**Author:** Competitor Tracker & Co. (verified)

## Key Features

- **Weekly crawling:** Automated weekly scans of competitor websites
- **Change detection:** Identifies diffs in pricing, product pages, messaging, and corporate content
- **Tagged reports:** Changes are categorized and ranked by significance
- **MCP-native:** Query competitor intel directly from Claude, Cursor, ChatGPT, and any MCP client
- **Verified listing:** Published on mcp.so with verified status

## Business Relevance

Competitive intelligence for operators who need to stay ahead without manual tracking. Use cases:
- **Pricing intelligence:** Detect when competitors change pricing — catch price increases, new tiers, or discount strategies
- **Product monitoring:** Track new feature launches, product page updates, and positioning shifts
- **Messaging analysis:** Identify changes in competitor value propositions, taglines, and market positioning
- **Strategic awareness:** Weekly reports provide regular competitive landscape updates without manual effort
- **Sales enablement:** Arm your sales team with current competitive intelligence pulled by your AI agent

Essential for operators in product management, marketing, sales, and strategy roles who need systematic competitive monitoring.

## Integration with CorpusIQ

Pair with CorpusIQ's analytics connectors to correlate competitor movements with your own performance data. Ask: "Competitor X dropped pricing by 20% this week — did our conversion rate change?" Combine competitor intelligence with GA4, Stripe, and Shopify data for complete market awareness.

## Limitations

- Weekly cadence (not real-time) — breaking changes may not appear for up to 7 days
- Depends on publicly accessible web pages — gated content is invisible
- Crawl accuracy depends on site structure stability
- Web-only — no social media, review site, or dark social monitoring
